--- layout: default title: External resources ---

External resources

Articles

  • A short essay by Helen Zhao, on a radical analysis of science.
  • A short opinion article by Erle C. Ellis on science, global change and planetary boundaries.
  • An article by G.A. Cohen on promoting egalitarianism while belonging to a privileged collective.

Webpages of Scientists I admire

  • Chanda Prescod Weinstein's website with her experience on Theoretical Physics & Black Feminist Science and Society
  • Julia Steinberger's website is an inspiration for action on climate change
  • Yarden Katz's website contains resources on several topics that move me
  • Donald Knuth's website is a gold mine of common sense

Organizations

  • Science for the People The organization Science for the people and its magazine promote well-thought content on the way the scientific system works, highlighting its problems and providing guidance into how we can improve it, in such a way that science has a positive impact for all people. In fact, although it could lead to a utopian society, science is too often used to oppress minorities, increase inequality, and violate the environment.
  • Scientist Rebellion The organization Scienctist Rebellion is an international scientists' environmentalist group that campaigns for degrowth, climate justice, and more effective climate change mitigation. You can join the Zurich Chapter by emailing scientistrebellion_ch@protonmail.ch
